dailyNews.title: Boots on ground may not be needed: Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Iran war
dailyNews.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio stated the U.S. aims to dismantle Iran's missile, drone, naval, and air force capabilities without ground troops, expecting operations to conclude in weeks. He clarified recent troop deployments offer President Trump options for contingencies, not for direct engagement in the conflict.

dailyNews.title: Alleged Bomb Plot Against NYC Pro-Palestinian Activist Thwarted
dailyNews.description: Federal and New York City authorities arrested a man this week who they allege plotted to firebomb the Staten Island home of a pro-Palestinian activist, the latest in a string of politically motivated violence cases in the city.

dailyNews.title: 'Bibi Really Sold It': A Rebuke In Vance-Netanyahu Tense Call Amid Iran War
dailyNews.description: Another senior US official told Axios that, in their view, Vance remained the most viable path to a negotiated end to the war. "If the Iranians can't strike a deal with Vance, they don't get a deal....

dailyNews.title: Two Cuba-bound aid ships missing after leaving Mexico
dailyNews.description: Mexico's navy launches a rescue mission for ships delivering aid to Cuba, strained by food and fuel shortages amid a US embargo.

dailyNews.title: Mexico, Cuba Searching for Missing Aid Vessels as Island Reels
dailyNews.description: Mexico and Cuba are trying to find two small vessels that were delivering aid to the island as part of growing solidarity missions in the face of heightened US pressure on the government in Havana.

dailyNews.title: JD Vance leads first anti-fraud task force meeting on social programmes
dailyNews.description: Vance, speaking Friday before the task force held a closed-door meeting, said that the federal government for decades had not taken the issue of fraud seriously and that it needed to be tackled with ‘a whole-government approach.’

dailyNews.description: Five Guys said the bonuses are meant to recognise the "grit and dedication" of its employees

dailyNews.title: ‘Humanitarian catastrophe’: While the world is focused on the war in Iran, something much worse is unfolding in Lebanon
dailyNews.description: Following the US-Israeli Operation Epic Fury against Iran, Israel's ground invasion of Lebanon has led to significant civilian casualties and displacement. UNICEF reports over 370,000 children have been uprooted, with many facing dire living conditions amid ongoing violence and humanitarian crises.

dailyNews.title: Congress releases list of 16 candidates for upcoming Puducherry elections
dailyNews.description: DPR Selvam will contest from Mannadipattu, while P Karthikeyan has been fielded from Oosudu.

dailyNews.title: What We Know About Pay for T.S.A. Workers
dailyNews.description: President Trump on Friday directed the Homeland Security Department to pay thousands of airport security officers.

dailyNews.title: At CPAC, the ‘America First’ Crowd Ponders Giving War a Chance
dailyNews.description: The Conservative Political Action Conference has been an America First gathering for years, but with President Trump pursuing war in Iran, organizers have tried to make a shift, with mixed results.

dailyNews.title: Outside Kennedy Center, Jane Fonda and Joan Baez Raise Voices in Protest
dailyNews.description: The pair joined in a gathering of artists and others who denounced censorship and faulted President Trump’s growing influence over the nation’s cultural life.

dailyNews.title: Kennedy Center Performer Asks Judge to Toss Case He Calls ‘Retaliatory’
dailyNews.description: A jazz drummer who called off a performance in protest of the center’s being renamed for President Trump said the president’s allies had sued him as punishment.

dailyNews.title: One Republican Farmer in Wisconsin Is Bitter Over Trump’s Deportations
dailyNews.description: For 20 years, Tim O’Harrow has asked lawmakers to acknowledge that America’s food supply depends on immigrants. Politics went in the other direction.

dailyNews.title: Ambitious, authoritarian and talking to Trump? The hard-liners rising in Iran
dailyNews.description: The Israeli military has systematically killed Iran’s top leaders, so when President Donald Trump said this week that talks are being held with Iran, the biggest question was: Who exactly is the U.S. talking to

dailyNews.title: Markets plunge and U.S. oil hits $100 as Trump's attempt to reassure Wall Street fails
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump is used to bending financial markets to his will

dailyNews.title: Iran war rages, Strait of Hormuz still blocked as Trump pushes for a deal
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration expects Iran's formal response to its 15-point peace proposal today, as Tehran continues blocking the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: House GOP attempts to forge own path toward ending shutdown, disregarding Senate-backed bill
dailyNews.description: House lawmakers are at odds over a Senate bill that includes TSA pay, but excludes ICE funding. Eleanor Mueller, White House economic policy reporter for Semafor, and Riley Rogerson, House leadership reporter for Politico, join "The Takeout" to discuss.

dailyNews.title: What methods is Trump using to try to negotiate with Iran?
dailyNews.description: The U.S. is waiting for Iran's response to a 15-point outline of a peace deal that was presented on Thursday, with Pakistan acting as an intermediary. CBS News national security contributor Sam Vinograd has more.

dailyNews.title: House Republicans reject Senate bill to fund DHS amid shutdown
dailyNews.description: The Department of Homeland Security said TSA employees could begin receiving paychecks again as early as Monday, after President Trump signed an executive order to restore their pay. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has more from Capitol Hill.
